title: Laundry Renovations
heroAlt: Custom laundry renovation with built-in cabinetry and stone benchtop — InsideOut Joinery Sydney
metaTitle: Laundry Renovations Sydney | Custom Joinery | InsideOut Joinery & Renos
metaDescription: Custom laundry renovations across Sydney. Proper storage, stone benchtops and cabinetry built for daily use. Free quotes. Call InsideOut Joinery on 02 5000 0402.
intro: The laundry gets used every day and renovated almost never. Most of the laundries we walk into are the same — washing machine on one side, not enough bench space, nowhere logical to put anything, and cabinetry that has been collecting moisture damage for a decade. A well-designed laundry does not need to be large to work well. It needs the right amount of bench, the right storage, and joinery that is built to handle the conditions. We can fix yours.
includedTitle: What we can take care of
included: Custom overhead and under-bench cabinetry designed around the actual dimensions of your laundry
Appliance bays for front-loader or top-loader configurations — side by side or stacked depending on what the space allows
Stone or laminate benchtops on moisture-resistant substrate built for a room that gets wet
Trough selection and plumbing coordination so the finished layout works with your existing service positions or the new ones
Integrated ironing board storage, pull-out hamper drawers and linen cupboard sections where the brief calls for it
Concealed cabinetry for homes where the laundry doubles as a mudroom or entry storage area

featureBlocks.title: More bench space than you think you need
featureBlocks.text: Ask anyone who has renovated a laundry what they would do differently and the answer is almost always: more bench. It is used for sorting, folding, treating stains, putting things down while you load the machine. We plan bench runs generously and make sure the height works for the people using it — not just for the standard cabinet module underneath.

featureBlocks.title: Joinery that handles moisture
featureBlocks.text: A laundry takes more moisture than almost any other room in the house. Substrate choice, finish type and how cabinets are sealed at the base all affect how long the joinery lasts. We do not cut corners here because the problems show up fast — within a couple of years of a poorly specified laundry fitout you will start to see swelling, warping and peel. We use materials and methods that hold up.

featureBlocks.title: Small room, lots of decisions
featureBlocks.text: Laundries are small and almost every decision in a small room matters. Where the trough sits relative to the machine, whether you have a single deep drawer or two shallower ones, whether the overhead cabinets go full height or leave a shelf gap — these are the details that make the difference between a laundry that is good to use and one that is just better looking than what was there before.

materialsTitle: What we typically spec in a laundry fitout
materials: Moisture-resistant board substrate as the base for all cabinetry — not standard MDF, which fails quickly in wet environments
Two-pac polyurethane or high-pressure laminate door finishes, both of which handle humidity well
Stone benchtops for clients who want a premium finish and a surface that does not stain or warp
Laminate benchtops in a solid colour or stone-look finish — a practical choice for laundries where budget is better spent elsewhere
Stainless steel or stone laundry troughs in undermount and freestanding configurations
Soft-close cabinetry hardware as standard — hinges and drawer runners that do not rattle or slam in a room that gets daily use

beforeAfter.title: Before & after
beforeAfter.text: The Epping project included a laundry fitout that turned an underused, poorly planned room into proper functional storage. Same footprint — completely different daily experience.
beforeAfter.afterAlt: Epping laundry after renovation — custom cabinetry, stone benchtop and organised storage

faqs.question: How much does a laundry renovation cost?
faqs.answer: Most laundry renovations sit between $5,000 and $15,000 depending on the size of the room, the benchtop material and whether plumbing needs to move. We give you a detailed quote after seeing the brief — the range is wide because laundries vary a lot.

faqs.question: How long does a laundry renovation take?
faqs.answer: Most laundry fitouts are completed in one to three weeks. If plumbing is being relocated or the room is being fully stripped and rebuilt, add a bit more time. We give you a clear timeline before starting.

faqs.question: Can you work with an existing trough position, or do you move it?
faqs.answer: Both. We can design the layout around your existing plumbing to keep costs down, or we can move the trough and waste if a different position makes more sense for how the room should work. We will tell you honestly which approach suits your situation.

faqs.question: Can the laundry also work as a mudroom or storage for the entry?
faqs.answer: Yes — this is a common brief, particularly in homes where the laundry is near the back door. We can integrate coat hooks, shoe storage, bag shelving and utility cabinetry into the same fitout so the space handles more than just washing.

faqs.question: Do you manage the plumbing as part of the project?
faqs.answer: Yes. We coordinate the plumber as part of the renovation so the cabinetry and service positions are sequenced correctly. You do not need to separately organise a plumber and then a joiner.

ctaText: Send us a photo of the existing space and tell us what is not working. We will scope the job and quote it properly — no guesswork.
